title: Serving my app from monday.com (deploy via upload) not working correctly
excerpt:
Hi there, 
So I have this strange problem: if I deploy my app from an URL and publish it, everything works correctly. If I upload a ZIP file via the form and serve that version from the monday.com cdn, it doesn’t render …

posts.author: kioleanu (Viorel Sfetea)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi there,

So I have this strange problem: if I deploy my app from an URL and publish it, everything works correctly. If I upload a ZIP file via the form and serve that version from the monday.com cdn, it doesn’t render properly. As far as I can tell the files are being loaded correctly, but the app is not rendering.

Serving the application from other providers, like Netlify works without problems.

I have a suspicion it has something to do with React Router, which I use and which creates routes like /route1, /route2 etc, which need to be rewritten to index.html.

I’m not sure if the default behaviour should be rewriting all and any URLs to index.html, but my feeling tells me yes, since it’s expected that applications should be SPAs.

posts.author: dsilva (Daniel Silva)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @kioleanu - that’s an odd one for sure. Let me check in with the team on this and get back to you.

Best,
Daniel

posts.author: cryptocharlie404 (charlie)
posts.cookedHtml: Have you tried using hashrouter instead of browserrouter? Don’t know what you’re doing but if your urls look like that, it could be choking on the history API.

posts.author: kioleanu (Viorel Sfetea)
posts.cookedHtml:
cryptocharlie404:

Don’t know what you’re doing but if your urls look like that, it could be choking on the history API.

That is unlikely. The app is working correctly on multiple other servers. The only thing that changes is from where the files are served

posts.author: dipro (Dipro Bhowmik)
posts.cookedHtml:
Asked in slack: Slack

I think we should ask for a console screenshot to see if there are any errors.

The history API that cryptocharlie is referring to is a mechanism to modify the URL without actuually moving to another page: Using the HTML5 History API | CSS-Tricks - CSS-Tricks.

posts.author: cryptocharlie404 (charlie)
posts.cookedHtml:
it worked for this user

Uploaded build, but cannot see it work in a board view monday Apps & Developers
Hi @dsilva thanks for the welcome! As an update: I figured it out. Using HashRouter instead of BrowserRouter does the trick. For anyone out there also having similar issues with in-app routing on apps deployed on monday.com!

what does your generated url look like?

posts.author: system (system)
posts.cookedHtml: This topic was automatically closed 7 days after the last reply. New replies are no longer allowed.
